commit.message:
Simplify inferred review metadata

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Consolidate+Fix demo-docs build + GH Pages deploy (#137)

- Consolidate demo docs build and GitHub Pages deployment into a single workflow
  with separate assemble (PR-safe) and deploy (push-only) stages
- Ensure correct permissions scoping (contents: read for build, pages/id-token for deploy)
- Add combined-site artifact for every run to enable preview without deployment
- Enable pip caching and simplify CI setup

- Introduce MkDocs-based portal as the main documentation entrypoint
  - Render README as landing page
  - Provide navigation to Sphinx and MkDocs demo builds
  - Ensure compatibility for both deployed site and local file:// browsing
    (explicit .html paths, redirect handling, no directory URLs)

- Standardize documentation structure:
  - Move MkDocs config to repository root (mkdocs.yml)
  - Organize demo sources under demo-docs/{sphinx,mkdocs}
  - Align paths across CI, docs, and project layout

- Improve demo documentation:
  - Rewrite demo index pages with proper package introduction
  - Replace static portal with dynamic MkDocs project
  - Update screenshots and fix inaccurate examples

- Add and refine Sphinx and Griffe extensions:
  - Ensure proper docstring rendering (e.g. deprecated directives)
  - Improve robustness (logging, guards, mypy compatibility)
  - Add integration and unit tests for extensions

- Clean up project configuration:
  - Exclude demo/build artifacts from tests, packaging, and tooling
  - Update ignore patterns and paths (demo-* conventions)
  - Remove obsolete files, duplicate structures, and unused dependencies

- Minor improvements:
  - Simplify theme dependencies (use built-in themes)
  - Update contributing docs and project structure references

---------

commit.message:
Fix stale event identity after board resubscribe

When the board reconnects after auth/session churn, local unconfirmed events could remain stamped with the previous session and author identity. The client would then keep retrying that stale pending batch after BoardSubscriptionCompleted, which matches the server-side failures where authorUserId no longer matches the authenticated user and the resend sequence drifts out of sync.

Refresh the identity on every unconfirmed local event as soon as the subscription handshake completes, and drop any stale pending send state before publish timers resume. This preserves local edits while ensuring the next outbound batch is rebuilt with the current connection sessionId and authorUserId.

Add a regression test covering the resubscribe path and keep the existing router/log tests green.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Keep interoperability roadmap lanes explicit until support is proven (#45)

The current mainline docs still left Aerial, cuMAC, and broader profile
expansion easy to read as general future support rather than bounded,
reviewable roadmap lanes. This patch updates the README and architecture
docs to separate hardened-now contract scaffolding from roadmap-only
interoperability claims, and adds a focused regression test so the
wording does not drift.

Constraint: Repo-visible docs must distinguish hardened-now support from roadmap-only claims
Rejected: Rely on issue tracker links alone | repo docs would still overstate support boundaries
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Do not promote Aerial, cuMAC, or broader-profile claims without updating these docs and the replacement contract docs test together
Tested: rg claim inventory across touched docs and the regression test; mix test apps/ran_action_gateway/test/ran_action_gateway/replacement_contract_examples_test.exs; mix contract_ci; git diff --check
Not-tested: GitHub PR checks on the fresh branch
Related: YON-56
Related: YON-58
Related: YON-59
Related: YON-60

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
チャレンジ共通ロジックと作成フォームを整理

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

security(proxy): add Origin-based CSRF protection in middleware (#899)

Replace per-route checkCsrfProtection() token pattern with a single
Origin header check in the middleware layer. This is the 2026 best
practice: modern browsers always send Origin on cross-origin mutation
requests, and SameSite=Lax cookies (Supabase default) provide
defense-in-depth.

Benefits:
- Covers ALL API routes automatically (vs ~100 individual calls)
- Zero client-side changes (no buildCsrfHeaders() wiring needed)
- No risk of breaking callers that don't send x-csrf-token
- Existing per-route checks remain as redundant defense-in-depth

Skip list: Bearer auth (mobile), webhooks, auth callbacks, cron jobs,
public analytics. Custom merchant domains are verified via domain cache.

Closes #898 (replaced approach — that PR would have broken logout and
loyalty redemption by requiring CSRF tokens callers don't send).

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: release v1.6.0 with richer schema extraction and field errors

Release focus

- release 1.6.0
- keep the release notes focused on the key changes
- commit with co-author
- push the gem and GitHub release after the commit

Key changes

- expand ActiveRecord schema extraction with except, on, required, and merge
- extract presence into allow_blank: false without overloading required semantics
- support field-level error and error_message overrides for validation and coercion failures
- keep merge keyed by raw field names and let merged required=false remove bang-style output names
- sync README and README_zh with the updated field option and schemas docs

Verification

- bundle exec rspec

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat: expand ActiveRecord schema extraction controls

User requests

- schemas add except option
- check whether ActiveRecord validation presence and allow_blank are extracted
- our gem allows changing the semantics of required, so keep that in mind when mapping presence
- check how validations with if and unless are handled
- schemas add an on option so validate on can be extracted
- schemas add a required option that can be true, false, or an array
- when required is an array, only those fields are required and every other field is treated as non-required
- document that rule in the README
- commit with co-author

Implementation

- add schemas(except: ...) and keep it composable with only:
- extract presence into allow_blank: false and avoid overloading it into required semantics alone
- skip validators with if:/unless: during static schema extraction
- support schemas(on: ...) so on:/except_on: validators can be extracted for a requested validation context
- add schemas(required: true | false | [fields]) as an explicit requiredness override for exported fields
- update README and README_zh with the new schemas options and semantics
- expand ActiveRecord schema specs to cover filtering, contexts, required overrides, and validator extraction edge cases

Verification

- bundle exec rspec

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(confluence-cli): 改进 Markdown 发布与附件处理 (#35)

## Why
- 当前 `confluence-cli` 的发布链路对 Confluence Data Center / Server
的行为控制不够直接，附件更新和页面更新流程也不够稳定。
- 需要补齐 Markdown 到 Confluence storage 的转换覆盖，并补充相关回归测试。

## What
- 将 `confluence_api_client` 改为基于 `httpx` 的 REST
封装，显式处理认证、页面版本递增、附件创建与附件更新。
- 重写 `publish-markdown` 的 Markdown 转换流程，基于 `markdown-it-py`
支持列表、表格、围栏代码块、本地附件链接和本地图片，并在附件上传后回填最终链接。
- 新增 `confluence_api_client` 与 `markdown_to_storage` 测试，并补充
`create-skill` / `gitlab-cli` 的文档说明。

## Testing
- `uv run --with httpx --with markdown-it-py --with pydantic --with rich
--with typer python -m unittest
skills.confluence-cli.scripts.tests.test_confluence_api_client
skills.confluence-cli.scripts.tests.test_markdown_to_storage`

## Risks
- Breaking change: `publish-markdown` 现在仅支持 `storage`，若仍传入其他
`body_format` 会直接报错，需要同步调整调用方式。

---------

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix: refine toc hover animation

Co-Authored-By: Codex GPT-5 <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat: refine blank coercion and required blank defaults

User requests

- Check whether allow_blank still errors on coercion failures such as Date with an empty string.
- allow_blank itself means the value is allowed and should not error.
- For empty values that cannot be converted, set them to nil.
- When a field allows blank, type coercion should not fail just because the input is an empty string.
- Add a setting for whether required fields allow_blank, default allow_blank=true.

Implementation

- Normalize blank-string coercion so meaningful blank values stay as-is while non-meaningful typed blanks become nil.
- Add config.required_allow_blank with field-level blank / allow_blank overrides.
- Update README and README_zh and cover the behavior with validator and ActiveRecord schema specs.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(mcp-core): Expose get_sentry_resource by default (#852)

Promote get_sentry_resource to the stable tool surface and hide the
legacy issue and trace detail tools behind it.

This fixes the confusion from GH-850 where get_sentry_resource and
breadcrumbs were only reachable in experimental mode. The legacy detail
handlers still exist so get_sentry_resource can compose them internally,
but they are now marked internal-only and filtered out of server
registration, generated definitions, plugin allowedTools, and
use_sentry's embedded tool surface.

I also updated public guidance and plugin prompts to point to
get_sentry_resource instead of the legacy tool names, and regenerated
the derived definitions so the stable and experimental plugin manifests
stay in sync.

Fixes GH-850

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(junior): Lazy-init runtime for route builds (#125)

Avoid build-time runtime initialization in the Junior route handlers and
keep the example app covered in CI.

Next was evaluating the queue callback route during build, which pulled
in the production Slack runtime at module load. That forced
`SLACK_SIGNING_SECRET` and `REDIS_URL` to exist during `next build`,
even though those are runtime requirements.

This change moves production app assembly behind lazy getters so the
production Slack bot, runtime, and Redis-backed state adapter are only
created when a request needs them. The queue callback and webhook
handlers now rely on those getters, which keeps route-module import safe
during `next build`. It also restores the missing `cli/env` tsup entry
so the published `junior` bin can still run `snapshot create`.

The PR also keeps the example app covered in CI by running `pnpm exec
next build` from `apps/example`. That checks the representative Next
build path without introducing an example-only build wrapper or
requiring snapshot sandbox auth in CI.

I considered removing the runtime config checks entirely, but that
weakens real request-time validation. The problem was the lifecycle
boundary, not the checks themselves.

---------

Co-authored-by: GPT-5 Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(tui): show more issues in the inline list

Summary:
- render issue list entries as single-line summaries that keep
  identifier, state, and title together
- update the visible-row calculation and add coverage for the denser
  default inline layout
- document the compact issue-row behavior in the FrankenTUI UI notes

Rationale:
- the default 22-row inline TUI only showed six issues because each
  issue consumed two rendered rows
- compact rows improve scanability without taking space away from the
  timeline or detail panes

Tests:
- cargo test -p opensymphony-tui
- cargo test -p opensymphony-cli --test tui

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(deps): patch picomatch and yaml vulnerabilities (#897)

Override picomatch to >=4.0.4 (ReDoS + method injection, high+medium)
and yaml to >=2.8.3 (stack overflow via deep collections, medium).
Both are transitive dev deps via @unlighthouse/cli.

Resolves Dependabot alerts #95, #96, #97, #98, #99.

commit.message:
fix(openhands): recreate sessions on LLM config drift

Summary:
- persist a hashed LLM config fingerprint in conversation manifests and
  compare it against the current workflow-resolved OpenHands request
  before reusing a conversation
- hard-recreate drifted conversations with DELETE plus same-ID POST,
  then recover context by appending Linear Codex workpad state to the
  first full prompt of the recreated fresh thread
- add Linear workpad comment lookup, CLI runtime wiring, fake-server
  delete coverage, and docs for the new recovery path

Rationale:
- same-ID POST preserves OpenHands history but does not update stored
  provider config, so credential rotation needs an explicit delete and
  recreate flow to avoid stale auth failures
- the manifest fingerprint avoids storing provider secrets in plaintext
  while still letting the runner detect model or env-value drift before
  attach and preserve issue-level continuity through the Linear workpad

Tests:
- cargo test -p opensymphony-openhands
- cargo test -p opensymphony-linear
- cargo test -p opensymphony-cli
- cargo clippy -p opensymphony-openhands -p opensymphony-linear -p opensymphony-cli --tests -- -D warnings

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor(provider): migrate OpenAI transport to responses

Summary:
- switch the OpenAI provider from /chat/completions to /responses
  and translate conversation history into Responses input items
- encode assistant tool calls and tool results as Responses
  function_call and function_call_output items
- update provider tests and README wording to match the Responses
  API transport

Rationale:
- align the transport layer with OpenAI's current recommended
  Responses API and function-calling model
- keep the existing agent-core conversation and tool abstractions
  by translating them at the provider boundary
- validate the streaming parser against Responses event types
  instead of Chat Completions deltas

Tests:
- ./gradlew test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* feat(jumia): rewrite Jumia integration for Vendor Center API

Rewrite catalog, feeds, fulfillment, orders, and product routes
to align with the Jumia Vendor Center API. Adds TDD test coverage
for all new schemas, submodules, routes, and components.

- New Zod schemas for all Vendor Center API responses
- Split client.ts into submodules: catalog, orders, fulfillment, feeds, helpers
- Add forIntegration() factory for multi-shop support
- V2 pack endpoint, simplified RTS/cancel/print-labels
- Auto-paginating getAllProducts/getAllOrders/getAllCategories/getAllBrands
- Wire JUMIA_ENVIRONMENT through env.ts validation
- Edge function: chunked queries, upsert-gated sync cursor
- Components: brand-selector caching, category-selector flat list, price-form Intl formatting
- 3923 tests pass, 0 lint errors, 0 type errors, 0 CodeRabbit findings

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(sandbox): Restore ambient Vercel auth resolution (#123)

Restore the sandbox auth behavior we had before the recent
sandbox/runtime refactors.

The sandbox credential helper started treating `VERCEL_TEAM_ID` and
`VERCEL_PROJECT_ID` as an incomplete explicit credential set, which made
Vercel prod fail before `@vercel/sandbox` could resolve ambient auth.
This change defers back to the SDK when running inside Vercel and keeps
explicit token validation for non-Vercel environments.

I also loaded `.env*` for local `pnpm exec junior ...` flows so `junior
snapshot create` and `junior check` match the existing repo wrapper
during local development. I considered removing the helper entirely, but
we still want the clearer failure for real partial token-based
configuration outside Vercel. Added focused unit coverage for both
paths.

---------

Co-authored-by: GPT-5 Codex <codex@openai.com>
